title: Partner Platform (B2P) Redesign
subtitle: Modernizing an outdated system to reduce manual work and improve partner experience
challenge: The Partner Platform (B2P) had grown organically over years into a fragmented, outdated system. The users, electricians, were daily spending unneccesary time on manual data entry, and the lack of self-service capabilities forced support teams to handle routine requests. The system's complexity led to high error rates, user frustration, and significant operational cost — requiring a full redesign that balanced user needs with internal feasibility.

designProcess.intro: I led the end-to-end design process — from initial research through developer collaboration and testing. Close involvement of developers ensured feasibility while maintaining design integrity across the platform.

steps.title: Research & Discovery
steps.description: Led user mapping sessions, conducted stakeholder interviews across product, operations, and support teams, and facilitated workshops to understand user pain points. Analyzed current workflows and identified bottlenecks through contextual inquiry.

steps.title: Strategy & Planning
steps.description: Conducted comprehensive impact mapping to understand business goals and user needs. Prioritized features based on strategic importance and developed a phased release plan that balanced quick wins with long-term platform improvements.

steps.title: Design & Testing
steps.description: Created interaction prototypes and conducted iterative testing with real users. Close collaboration with developers ensured feasibility of designs while maintaining design integrity. Multiple rounds of user testing refined the experience.

insights: Users spent excessive hours on daily manual data entry, applying multiple cases for the same customer, representing massive efficiency opportunity
Users need clear guidance as there are many niche cases that look similar to correctly perform their tasks
Not auto-filling data when possible and executing multiple errands in one submission were key pain points for users
Users needed manage several accounts to perform their tasks - causing frustration among users as well as support teams

deliverables.intro: The redesigned Partner Platform featured new company accounts types, enabling users to only need one personal account to manage all their company's cases. An intuitive dashboard providing quick guidance to most common cases and functionalities. The new case selection flow ensures that users are guided through the process of choosing the correct case and submitting the correct information. The new design system ensured consistency and scalability across the product portfolio.

images.alt: Expanded design system - built with product portfolio in mind.

results.intro: The redesigned experience delivered measurable improvements across key metrics.

kpis.label: Manual work reduction
kpis.value: Data pending. (early feedbacks show significant improvement)
